chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Sales Order
triplets.object: customer, items, payment status, delivery information

triplets.subject: Order Line Item
triplets.object: Unit price at time of order

triplets.subject: Unit price
triplets.object: does not change with future price updates

triplets.subject: Return Policy
triplets.object: return within 30 days of purchase for a full refund

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition: A data retention rule specifying that purchase-related records are retained for seven years after the last purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: A boolean attribute indicating whether the entity is active (true) or inactive (false), i.e., currently able to perform its primary action (such as placing orders or being offered for sale).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses
- Geographic region for tax calculation
- Active/inactive status | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | | IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether customer can place orders | NO | | | IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether currently for sale | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Category
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product Category is the entity representing a category within a hierarchical product taxonomy, including its identifier, display name, and its place in the category tree.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree. | | CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO | | **Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID) | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Belongs to exactly one Category | Category         → TB_CATEGORY

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master
entities_post_resolution.definition: A master data table containing a unique record for every registered platform customer, identified by the primary key CUST_ID.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers. | **Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | FULL_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Customer's full name | NO | | | EMAIL | VARCHAR(150) | Unique email address for login | NO | | | REGION_CODE | VARCHAR(10) | Geographic region for tax | YES | | | CREATED_AT | DATETIME | Account creation timestamp | NO | | | IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether customer can place orders | NO | | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | - CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A customer is an individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase and is the recipient of order-related actions such as refunds.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses
- Geographic region for tax calculation
- Active/inactive status | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer | A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | # Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

entities_post_resolution.name: Line 1
entities_post_resolution.definition: Line 1 represents the product item Wireless Headphones, referenced in the provenance as a purchasable item at retail (€79.99 per unit).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Examples**:
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50

entities_post_resolution.name: Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A line item is an entry on a sales order that references exactly one product and specifies the quantity and related attributes for that product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Examples**:
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item.Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: An Order Line Item represents an individual product line within a sales order, identified by LINE_ID and linked to the order via ORDER_ID and to the product via PRODUCT_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | **Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------
| WAREHOUSE_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | Source warehouse | YES | | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | **Key Attributes**:
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | An Order Line Item

entities_post_resolution.name: Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A purchase transaction in an e-commerce system representing a customer order, which can be cancelled and refunded.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled. | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments.

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique product identifier (PRODUCT_ID) in the TB_PRODUCT table, representing a single product in the master product catalog.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | | PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES | | - PARENT_CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID) [self-reference] | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree. | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO | | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | **Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID) | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A payment transaction recorded for a sales order, identified by a unique payment identifier and containing the payment amount.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Payment amount | **Key Attributes**:
- Confirmation timestamp | A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A catalog item representing a saleable product in the e-commerce system, identified by a unique product_id and described by a human-readable product_name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique SKU code | **Key Attributes**:
- Human-readable product name | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Key Attributes**:
- Belongs to exactly one Category | **Business Rules**:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold | **Business Rules**:
- Prices can change daily based on market conditions | **Business Rules**:
- Inactive products cannot be ordered but remain for historical reference | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Product          → TB_PRODUCT | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale. | **Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | | PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO | | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Sales Order Header is the master record for a customer's order, identified by ORDER_ID, and includes the associated customer ID (CUST_ID) while serving as the parent for line items and payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is a fulfillment entity representing delivery of goods for a single Sales Order, identified by a unique shipment ID and including ship timestamp and tracking number.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Tracking number | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------
| SHIPPED_AT | DATETIME | Ship timestamp | YES | | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------
| TRACKING_NUMBER | VARCHAR(100) | Carrier tracking number | YES | | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: The unit price is the price per single unit of an item, expressed in EUR, and can refer to the current price or to the price at the time of an order (historical).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: Multiple Addresses
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type

entities_post_resolution.name: Not Yet Shipped
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Can only be cancelled if not yet shipped
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can only be cancelled if not yet shipped

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity × Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Valid Category
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer records including identifiers, contact details, regional grouping, account status, and the timestamp when the record was created.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, naming, categorization, pricing, and availability status.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Contains the hierarchical categories used to classify products or items, including a reference to a parent category for nested category structures.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores the high-level information for customer orders, including identifiers, dates, financial totals, status, payment confirmation, delivery timestamp, and record creation time.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores details of each product line within orders, including the products and quantities ordered and the line-level pricing.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments associated with customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments associated with customer orders, including warehouse, shipment timestamp, tracking details, and current shipment status.
